Notes: The original Shade (Alpha): {B}: +1/+1 until end of turn. Black creature; classic black +N/+N pump tied to black mana.
Red Aura Curse: enchanted player's creatures attack each combat if able. Red forced-attack.
White creature: exile a permanent, return it end of turn. Signature white flicker effect.
Red enchantment: your creatures have haste. Classic red haste-anthem.
Blue Aura preventing all combat damage to/from enchanted creature. The namesake Gaseous Form.
Red sorcery; untaps attackers and grants an additional combat phase (extra attack).
White instant: destroy target artifact or enchantment. Iconic enchantment removal in white.
Blue creature with Flash. Famous flash staple.
Green instant; puts a +1/+1 counter on a creature and grants hexproof until end of turn (hexproof).
Green Aura: all able creatures must block enchanted creature. Iconic forced-block.
White enchantment giving your creatures +1/+1. Classic anthem friendly to the creature type.
Green instant preventing all combat damage. The namesake Fog effect.
Green creature: {G} exiles a card from a graveyard. Premier green graveyard-hate staple.
Black instant: target player sacrifices a creature. Classic edict/forced-sacrifice.
White creature rewarding low-power creatures with card draw. White friendliness to a quality (small/cheap).
Black sorcery: control target player during their next turn. Black mind-control of a turn.
Blue instant: tap up to two creatures; they don't untap next turn. Blue freeze/tap-down.
White flying angel. Quintessential flier in white.
White creature with first strike. Iconic early first-strike body.
Green sorcery: your creature fights an enemy creature. Defining green fight spell.
Red sorcery; exile top two cards, play them this/next turn — quintessential impulsive draw.
Red creature; makes a 1/1 Elemental whenever you cast an instant or sorcery.
Blue sorcery; take an extra turn — flagship blue time manipulation.
White enchantment; each player limited to one spell per turn — white-style lockdown.
Red Aura Curse; enchanted player's creatures attack each combat if able — red 'must attack'.
Black creature; opponents lose life equal to your black devotion — life loss as an effect (drain).
Red enchantment; grants menace to your creatures — classic menace enabler.
Blue enchantment; opponents play with hands revealed — pure 'look at opponent's hand'.
White Angel; grants indestructible to all your permanents — flagship indestructible card.
Red sorcery; destroy target land — the archetypal land destruction spell.
Black enchantment; draw an extra card but lose 1 life each upkeep — life loss as an ongoing cost.
Green Aura; all creatures able to block enchanted creature must do so — the namesake Lure.
Blue instant; gain control of an Aura and reattach it — blue's enchantment-moving trick.
Blue Illusion creature; sacrificed when it becomes the target of a spell/ability — the defining Illusion drawback.
Green enchantment; doubles tokens and counters placed — iconic counter/token multiplier.
White Angel with flying, first strike, lifelink — premier white lifelink beater.
Black sorcery; name a card, remove all copies from hand, library and graveyard — iconic lobotomy effect.
Red sorcery; draw two then discard two — the namesake looting spell.
Colorless Equipment; all creatures able to block the single equipped creature must — Lure restricted to one creature.
Black instant; add BBB once — quintessential temporary (one-shot) mana ritual.
White creature; gain 1 life whenever another creature enters — classic incremental life gain.
Green creature; the namesake Lhurgoyf, P/T scales with card types in graveyards.
Blue creature; landfall mills three — iconic mono-blue mill engine.
Green creature; taps for permanent, repeatable green mana — iconic mana dork.
Green creature; P/T equal to cards in your hand — the literal namesake of the Maro ability.
Red Dinosaur; on attack exile top of EACH player's library and cast for free.
Red sorcery; discard two THEN draw three (rummaging: discard before draw).
White instant; return an artifact (or enchantment) card from GY to hand.
Black artifact; cast spells off your top paying life instead of mana (free of mana).
The namesake Blue sorcery; destroy a creature, dig to reveal a creature into play.
Red one-drop, the signature Prowess creature.
Red instant literally named Panic; target creature can't block this turn.
Red punisher: opponent chooses 5 damage to them or you draw three cards.
The namesake White Aura; enchanted creature can't attack or block.
The classic Black spell; return a creature card from GY to hand.
Classic Green Reach creature; can block fliers.
Staple Green sorcery; return up to three land cards from GY to hand.

White instant; opponents can't cast spells this turn (preventing actions).

Black Zombie; draw a card whenever a nontoken creature you control dies.

Blue enchantment; play lands and cast spells off your top, paying normal costs.

Five-color artifact; sacrifice to make target player lose (the opponent-can't-win line).
